scenarios.title: The Morning Chaos
scenarios.subtitle: Every morning is an event.
scenarios.prompt: It's a weekday morning. Kids need to get ready, lunches need to happen, and you need to get out the door. What does this look like?

choices.text: We have a routine. Everyone knows what to do and it mostly runs itself
I'm directing traffic the entire time. It works but I'm the engine
It's a scramble every single day and I don't know how we make it
My partner handles most of the morning. I deal with other parts

scenarios.title: The Dinner Question
scenarios.subtitle: The question that never stops.
scenarios.prompt: It's 4pm. Someone's going to ask 'what's for dinner?' What's going through your head?

choices.text: I already know. I plan meals for the week
I've got go-to meals and I'll figure it out in the next hour
I dread this question every single day
We order takeout more than I'd like to admit

scenarios.title: The Mental Load
scenarios.subtitle: The stuff nobody sees.
scenarios.prompt: Doctor appointments, school events, permission slips, birthday parties, groceries, that thing you were supposed to call about... How do you keep track?

choices.text: I have a system. Calendar, lists, reminders. A lot but organized
It's mostly in my head and that's terrifying
I write things down in 5 different places
My partner and I split it but things still fall through

scenarios.title: The Kid Moment
scenarios.subtitle: They need you right now.
scenarios.prompt: Your kid comes to you with something. A homework question, a social problem, a big feeling. What do you do?

choices.text: I try to help even if I have to quietly Google the answer
I sit with them and listen but I don't always know the right thing to say
I tell them to ask their teacher. I'm not equipped for everything
I feel guilty because I'm usually too tired to give them my full attention

scenarios.title: The Personal Time
scenarios.subtitle: Remember that?
scenarios.prompt: When was the last time you did something just for you? Not for the kids, not for work.

choices.text: I make time regularly. It keeps me sane
Occasionally but I always feel guilty about it
I honestly can't remember
Scrolling my phone after the kids are in bed counts, right?

scenarios.title: The Scheduling Nightmare
scenarios.subtitle: Five calendars, one family.
scenarios.prompt: Between your schedule, your partner's, kids' activities, and everything else. How does the family calendar work?

choices.text: Shared digital calendar. It's the source of truth
I keep track and tell everyone where to be
We try to coordinate but double-bookings happen regularly
We just wing it week by week

scenarios.title: The Household Admin
scenarios.subtitle: The never-ending list.
scenarios.prompt: Bills, groceries, home maintenance, car stuff, insurance, that thing you've been meaning to call about for two weeks. How's all that going?

choices.text: Mostly on autopilot. Auto-pay, recurring orders, maintenance schedule
I handle it as it comes up but stay mostly current
There are at least 3 things on that list I've been avoiding
My partner handles most of this

scenarios.title: The Worry
scenarios.subtitle: The thing that keeps you up.
scenarios.prompt: What's the thing about parenting that takes up more mental space than you'd like?

choices.text: Am I doing enough? Are my kids getting what they need from me?
Screen time, social media, technology. I worry about what it's doing to them
Money. Providing, saving for their future, affording activities
The balance. I feel like I'm failing at everything a little bit
